titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Terry Tempest Williams Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10122091.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 17, 2018 at 19:15

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, including manuscripts and proofs, journals, notebooks, day books, research files, photographs, printed materials, awards, artwork, and audiovisual materials related to the life and work of Terry Tempest Williams.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2011.
March 2013 Acquisition: gift of Terry Tempest Williams, 2013.

custodhist.head: Custodial History note
custodhist.p: Materials in Series I. Papers Prior to 2002 were previously on deposit at the J. Willard Marriott Library, University of Utah.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.
Boxes 153-154, 156, 357-359, 362: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 360-361: Restricted until January 1, 2037. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 161-162, 354-356 (audiovisual materials): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The Terry Tempest Williams Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Terry Tempest Williams Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Materials formerly on deposit at the Willard Marriott Library, University of Utah, were processed to a greater level of detail prior to receipt at the Beinecke Libary.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Terry Tempest Williams
bioghist.p: Terry Tempest Williams is an American writer, poet, naturalist, and environmental and social activist. Her writings link environmental causes with social justice and women's health, art, politics, and spirituality.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, including manuscripts and proofs, journals, notebooks, day books, research files, photographs, printed materials, awards, artwork, and audiovisual materials related to the life and work of Terry Tempest Williams.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: Series I. Papers Prior to 2002; Series II. Papers Chiefly After 2002; and March 2013 Acquisition.

custodhist.head: Custodial History note
custodhist.p: These materials were previously on deposit at the J. Willard Marriott Library, University of Utah.

processinfo.head: Processing Information note
processinfo.p: These papers were processed while on deposit at the J. Williard Marriott Library at the University of Utah. To the extent possible the arrangement, including box and folder numbers, was kept as received. Folder titles were not verified against the contents of each folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Also contains two other publications: NEO. (1988) and Ancient Forests on the Olympic National Forest (1990).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes an issue of American Way Magazine (November 1992), Utah Museum of Natural History correspondence and a McCall's Magazine typescript.

did.unittitle: Appearances, correspondence, declined events, and writings about Williams

arrangement.head: Arrangement note
arrangement.p: Roughly organized into the following groupings: Writings, Journals and Notebooks, Correspondence, Subject files and Miscellaneous papers, Photographs, Printed materials, Awards, Arwork and objects, Audiovisual materials, Restricted fragile, and Restricted papers. Because the papers received minimal arrangement below the box level, various types of materials can be found in multiple categories.

Utah Center for the Book Award photographs, posters, awards, photographs, "Open Space of Democracy" page proofs
Photographs, University of Utah photo and honorary degree album, red Sanibel and Captiva Islands bag, sea shell, pen
"The Illuminated Dessert" cover proofs, posters, Wilderness Society Award
Collage box, sea shells, photos, notebook given as a gift
Tile mosaic by Williams, "Detail: S. Apollinare Nuovo, Ravenna, 2002, Italia"
Palm leaf crown from FGCU fiasco, dried jelly fish with letter and photograph from Mama
Audiovisual materials, including VHS and audio cassettes
Audiovisual materials, including 2 micro-cassette recorders with tapes, 20 VHS cassettes, and 33 audio cassettes
Audiovisual materials, including VHS and audio cassettes

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until January 1, 2037. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Notes for a talk at the Beinecke Library on Rachel Carson's notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials from an appearence on Tim DeChristopher show, climate march, President Obama's inauguration. Also included are newspaper and magazine article, and interviews and ephemera from events and conferences.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: The Progressive magazines, drafts, and some correspondence. Also contains Andrea Lewis obituary and eulogy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: drafts, photographs, printed material, print-outs, notes, and various manuscript materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, writings and writings by others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: University of Utah 2003 Commencement information, an empty journal, slides and clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes related travel docuents and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes writings by others and conference papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: material regarding Chris Jordon's Midway, Galapagos, The Illustrated I Ching, and Richard Jefferies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a signed copy of the book, autographed on 11 Septmeber 2001.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials includeds are drafts, notes, notebooks, research, and edits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials included are notebooke and edited proofs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes: print-outs, conference packets, annotated books, magazines, newspaper clippings, an art collage and some drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes, notes, notbooks, newspaper clippings, travel documents and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: various printed material with annotations, photographs, conference packets, notebooks with plant pressings, and material relating to Art of Revolution - One Million Bones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: notes, clippings, correspondence, and conference packets with speech notes.

did.unittitle: Writings, printed materials, notebooks and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: field notes, family geneaology, printed material with annotations, Four Last Songs, calendar, notebooks, photographs, Rwanda bracelet, correspondence, lock of Terry Tempest Williams' hair, newspaper clippings, and a t-shirt with homemade art designs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: clippings and printed material.

did.unittitle: Writings by others, correspondence and ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: photographs, correspondence, various objects (including feathers and other material), Murie portfolio, poetry, and clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also included are date books and a weddings contacts file.

did.unittitle: When Women Were Birds, writing and related materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains: correspondence; Summer 2012 notebook; typed interviews; ephemera; postcards; newspapers; various typed nomination packets; Ghost Ranch packet; photographs; cards; a student collage; Frank Carter thesis defense; business cards; a magazine; various print-outs with and without corrections; Storytelling, Culture and the Future of Democracy bound conference booklet; conference ID badges; proofs; A Place For Wildlife 2014 Symposium packet.

did.unittitle: Unison: A Dialogue Between the Land and Its Children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inlcudes an edited draft and a gift version in a photo album to Terry's parents.

did.unittitle: Unison: A Dialogue Between the Land and Its Children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy created by Viking Press.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, notes, newspaper clippings, and ephemera
Printed material, including newspapers, clippings, magazines, and books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains: notes; catalogs; a daybook; postcards; a namebadge; a print-out of the Beinecke Terry Tempest Williams finding aid (circa 2011); correspondence; photocopies; drafts; Utah State University Honorary Degree of Doctor of Humanities; Compton Foundation meeting booklet (2011); G.E.O. Funder's guide; Environmental Humanities Graduate program at the University of Utah packet; Guilford College and Brigham Young University speaking packet (2011).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two Center for Biological Diversity condom packets, as well as a female condom.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of perishable materials may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Nature research, writings and related correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Much of the materials is realted to Spain and the Williams Mosaic.

did.unittitle: When Women Were Birds, feather mask

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, with handwritten corrections. Also contains correspondence, and a mailer from James Balog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: The Dixon Digest (1999); Orion magazine (2004); correspondence; clippings; The Winter-Harvest Manual (2001); catalogs; In The Beginning: Wyoming and The West, typescript; photocopies; headshots of Terry Tempest Williams for An Unspoken Hunger (1994); Jesus The Christ: Articles of Faith, by James E. Talmage (1974); various notes; mortarboard tassel; A Dream For Katy: A Celebration of Early Mormon Women, by Susan Howe (1992); Patriacrchs and Politics: The Plight of the Mormon Woman, by Marilyn Warenski, including marginalia and a Latter-Day Saints pamphlet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials with marginalia, notes and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials with marginalia, notes, ephemera and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: catalogs and periodicals; non-fiction nominees; When Women Were Birds notes; event material; print-outs of emails; newspaper clippings; business correspondence; speaking contracts; various drafts; photocopies; "Ocean World: An Ecological Musical," by Anne Conlon; "She Is What I Want to Be," poem dedicated to Terry Tempest Williams, by Annabell Milliken; wood chips.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two textiles, with printed designs: "Liguria - Le Cinque Terre" and "Coastal Birds, by Lamont."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: a draft of Terry Tempest Williams' Will and Declaration of Trust. Also contains an estate planning questionnaire.
